_doc: Daily market-state snapshot. Written by the close-of-market run, read by the next morning run (and the next close run for day-over-day deltas). Reasoning fuel — the reader never sees this file, only the conclusions drawn from it. Fields left null were not collected; collect them next run rather than guessing.
session_note: A tired, waiting-for-Micron tape that faded its own bounce — then the print detonated after the close. SPX opened green (7,370.88, above the 7,365.46 prior close), ran to 7,428.06 (poking but NOT reaching the ~7,450 flip), got rejected at resistance and faded post-lunch to close 7,358.22 (-0.10%), a hair BELOW the prior close — a failed relief bounce. Dow +0.35% (green) vs Nasdaq -0.43% (red): the value-over-growth rotation rolled on. Macro quietly improved all day (10Y 4.40%, WTI $69.87, gold -2.75%, VIX -4.4% to 18.63). THEN Micron reported AMC and blew the doors off: rev $41.5B vs ~$35.6B est, EPS $25.11 vs ~$20.60, FQ4 guide $50B ±$1B vs ~$42.9B Street, plus 16 take-or-pay SCAs — stock +12% AH (briefly +16%). The disambiguator the whole week waited on landed decisively on the bull side.

spx.note: Open 7370.88, high 7428.06, low 7336.82, close 7358.22 (CBOE _SPX OHLC; close -0.10% / -7.24 vs 7365.46 prior). High-to-low range = 91.24 pts = 1.24%. Path: opened green above the prior close, rallied to 7428.06 — which TAGGED the broken hedge shelf / June-23 high zone (~7,424) but fell ~12-22 pts SHORT of the 7,440-7,450 flip — got sold there, and faded through the afternoon to close fractionally red, just above the 7,350 put wall band. A textbook unfueled bounce that died at resistance below the flip: the no-reclaim-7450 mechanism playing out (no mechanical dip-buying engine, no catalyst until MU after the close). MU then blew out AH — the gap risk for June 25 is now UP, the live test of no-reclaim-7450's falsifier.

nasdaq_composite.note: -0.43% to 25,476.64 — the laggard again as mega-cap growth/tech faded into the MU print; MU -0.31% regular session ($1,048.51), NVDA -0.52%, MRVL -0.84%, CRWV -4.58% (the day's worst portfolio name). The growth side stayed soft; the blowout that would rescue it didn't land until after the bell.

dow.note: +0.35% / +182.06 to 51,848.90 — GREEN. Dow up while Nasdaq down = the value-over-growth rotation continued for a third session; DELL +1.47% and TSM +1.02% the green portfolio standouts, AVGO +0.51% on the Jalapeño reveal. A green Dow on a red Nasdaq is the same chip/growth-specific de-risk character as June 23, just quieter.

after_hours_events.event: MICRON (MU) fiscal Q3 FY26 — record blowout, reported AMC June 24. Rev $41.5B (+346% YoY, +74% QoQ) vs ~$35.6B est; GM 84.9%; non-GAAP EPS $25.11 vs ~$20.60 est. FQ4 guide $50B ±$1B / ~86% GM / ~$31 EPS vs ~$42.9B Street. 16 multi-year take-or-pay SCAs (CY2026-2030, ~half of revenue when complete, price FLOORS locking GM 'well above any past peak'; 14/16 = ~$100B cumulative at minimum price; $22B deposits). Supply-demand reaffirmed tight 'beyond CY2027,' 'no line of sight' to supply catching demand. Stock +12% AH (briefly +16%).
after_hours_events.note: THE event of the week and the disambiguator for the June 23 rout. Resolves 2026-06-23-mu-guide-holds-demand RIGHT (done in the interactive ingest); pushes 2026-06-22-mu-sells-the-news (June 25 close < June 24 close) toward WRONG (a +12% AH gap argues for a higher June 25 close); and arms 2026-06-23-no-reclaim-7450's falsifier — a +12% memory-complex gap is exactly the catalyst that could carry SPX back over the 7,450 flip on June 25.

after_hours_events.event: Broadcom (AVGO) + OpenAI unveil 'Jalapeño,' OpenAI's first custom inference chip (the first in a multi-gen platform). 9-month design-to-tape-out, deploy end-2026, Microsoft to buy ~40%. AVGO +~3% intraday, closed +0.51% ($382.07).
after_hours_events.note: Validates the AVGO non-NVDA / custom-silicon hedge leg. BUT two same-day SemiWiki counterweights: (1) Broadcom reportedly LOST the Google TPU v9 SerDes socket to MediaTek (Google fell back from 448G to MediaTek's 336G on signal-integrity/thermal issues; AVGO's 448G timeline slipped); (2) Broadcom's 10-Q discloses a $29B contingent lease guarantee backstopping Apollo's purchase of OpenAI racks (balance-sheet subordination to OpenAI revenue — an ai-infrastructure-debt vector). Net: a headline win, a quieter socket loss, and a new contingent liability.

regime.rationale: Held at bull-under-stress, but the balance of risk shifted bullish AFTER the close. Through the regular session the negative-gamma read persisted: SPX closed 7,358.22, still BELOW the ~7,450 flip (net GEX still negative per snippet, -$49.4B carried from June 23 — no fresh compute, script failed a 5th day), so dealers stayed in amplify mode and the relief bounce died at resistance (high 7,428, rejected). What keeps it from a downgrade — and now tilts it toward healing: (1) the macro fear receded across the board (VIX -4.4% to 18.63, 10Y 4.40%, WTI sub-$70, gold -2.75% = orderly de-risk unwinding, not panic); (2) the 7,350 put wall held again (low 7,336.82, closed back above); (3) Dow green, breadth ex-tech fine — still a growth-specific stress, not broad risk-off; (4) and the disambiguator the whole tape was waiting on — Micron's guide — landed as a blowout, +12% AH. Per playbook (don't flip regime on one session; require a close beyond a named level), the call waits for a CLOSE back above 7,450 (heals it to bull) or below 7,300 (confirms bear).
regime.what_changes_it: Up to bull on a CLOSE above ~7,450 (reclaims the flip, re-stacks positive gamma) — the MU blowout is the catalyst that could do it as soon as June 25. Down to bear-rally/bear on a CLOSE below 7,300 (deeper put-wall magnet breaks, amplification toward 7,250/7,200), OR a hot May PCE (June 26) that hardens the BofA-75bp-hike narrative. Watch (1) whether MU's +12% AH gaps the memory/AI-hardware complex up enough to carry SPX over 7,450 June 25, (2) the 7,350 put wall on any retest, (3) Fed speakers vs the BofA hike call, (4) PCE June 26.

key_levels.notes: Support: 7,350 = put wall (today's low 7,336.82 dipped just below and reclaimed); 7,300 = prior aggregate put-wall magnet; 7,265 = June 10/11 hold. Resistance: 7,400 = peak-GEX magnet / broken hedge shelf (failed to hold today); 7,428 = today's high / rejection point; 7,450 = zero-gamma flip (THE reclaim line — above it dealers dampen again); 7,510 = broken-floor-now-ceiling. SPX still below the 7,450 flip = amplification zone, but the MU gap is the catalyst that could carry it over.

gamma.source: SNIPPET-PRIMARY per playbook rule (verdict 'suspect'), but the COMPUTED levels finally returned tonight after a long hang. market_levels.py --audit completed (~exit 0) very late in the run, verdict SUSPECT (1 FAIL of 7 checks: call-wall cross-source). The FLIP is now well-corroborated across all three feeds — snippet 7,450 / Polygon 7,477 / CBOE 7,479 (Polygon-vs-CBOE 0.03% apart) — so the real flip ZONE is ~7,450-7,480, with the snippet 7,450 at the low end. PUT WALL also tight: snippet 7,350 / Polygon 7,360 / CBOE 7,400 → ~7,350-7,360 band. The ONLY disagreement is the call wall: snippet 7,510 / Polygon 7,600 / CBOE 7,700 (the failing check). Snapshot keeps snippet headline numbers (flip 7,450, put 7,350, call 7,510) per the suspect-verdict rule; computed levels recorded in computed_rejected. Net GEX -$49.36B carried from June 23 snippet (no fresh same-day net-GEX number; treat magnitude as stale, sign negative — confirmed by the sub-flip close). Tape-coherence PASSES decisively: close 7,358.22 BELOW the 7,450-7,480 flip zone = negative gamma; the day's high 7,428 was rejected JUST BELOW the flip zone (resistance behaved exactly); the low 7,336.82 dipped to the 7,350-7,360 put-wall band and reclaimed (support behaved). near_call_wall set to the 7,450 flip (the operative intraday resistance that capped the bounce).
gamma.note: market_levels.py ran tonight (exit 0) after appearing hung for most of the run — a partial reprieve from the June 17-23 string of hard failures, though it still finished too late to be the primary and the call-wall check FAILED (Polygon 7,600 vs CBOE 7,700). Closes + OHLC obtained via the CBOE delayed_quotes JSON endpoint for all 11 holdings + SPY/QQQ/INTC/SOXX + _SPX/_VIX; factor_tape.py (Yahoo) ran fine for cross-asset. The flip/put-wall now agree across snippet + both computed feeds (the levels that matter for the tape), which materially raises confidence in the ~7,450-7,480 flip and ~7,350-7,360 put-wall band; only the far call wall is unreliable (and it's out of play below the flip anyway).

computed_rejected.note: Verdict suspect on the call-wall cross-source disagreement only. Flip (5/5 cross-checks pass, Polygon-CBOE 0.03% apart) and put wall are strongly corroborated; treat the flip as a ~7,450-7,480 ZONE and the put wall as ~7,350-7,360. The snapshot uses snippet headline numbers per the suspect-verdict fallback rule, but the computed flip/put-wall should be read as confirming, not contradicting, the snippet.

gamma.audit_override: Snapshot uses snippet headline numbers (flip 7,450 / put 7,350 / call 7,510) per the suspect-verdict rule; computed levels in computed_rejected. The flip and put wall are cross-source-corroborated (only the call wall failed), so confidence in the operative levels is HIGH despite the 'suspect' label — the suspect flag is entirely on the call wall, which is above the flip and out of play in a sub-flip tape.
gamma.audit_log: 2026-06-24: market_levels.py --audit launched ~17:00 CT, appeared hung (0 bytes) for most of the run, then COMPLETED (exit 0) late with verdict SUSPECT (6/7... actually 6 pass / 1 FAIL). Passes: flip-near-spot, vs-snippet-flip (computed 7477 vs snippet 7450, 0.36%), vs-snippet-call_wall (7600 vs 7510, 1.20%), vs-snippet-put_wall (7360 vs 7350, 0.14%), vs-cboe-flip (7477 vs 7479, 0.03%), vs-cboe-put_wall (7360 vs 7400, 0.54%). FAIL: vs-cboe-call_wall (7600 vs 7700, 1.30%). Closes/OHLC via CBOE delayed_quotes JSON (SPX open 7370.88 / high 7428.06 / low 7336.82 / close 7358.22; VIX 18.63). Cross-asset (factor_tape.py): VIX 18.63, 10Y 4.40, WTI 69.87, DXY 101.58, gold 4016.4. NOTE: this is the first session since June 16 the audit produced output at all — the retry-loop hang may be intermittent rather than terminal; keep launching it but keep the CBOE-delayed-quotes + snippet fallback as primary until it returns a clean 'pass' within the run window.

rates_commodities.note: 10Y 4.40% (-0.05, -1.1%, falling 5d) — eased for a second day, quietly cutting against the BofA '75bp of 2026 hikes' fear. WTI $69.87 (-4.56%, 5d -6.18 falling) — cracked under $70, war premium fully gone, a disinflationary tailwind that argues the rate-hike panic is overdone. Gold -2.75% to $4,016.40 (falling 5d, -314.5 over 5d) — gold DOWN again = the orderly dash-for-cash de-risk continues unwinding, not a flight to safety. DXY 101.58 (+0.17%, rising 5d) — firm dollar. From factor_tape.py (Yahoo). The whole cross-asset board is disinflationary/risk-stabilizing — the macro backdrop improved even as the equity bounce faded; the missing piece was a fundamental catalyst, which MU then supplied AH.

volatility_flow.vix_note: VIX -0.86 to 18.63 (-4.41%) — fear bled out for the first day since the rout, back below 19 and well off the June-10 war spike. A falling VIX on a flat-to-red SPX day = the panic is draining even though the index hasn't reclaimed the flip; consistent with an orderly, waiting-for-the-catalyst tape. From factor_tape.py.
volatility_flow.put_call_note: Not collected (lags 1-2 days free). Structural read: VIX -4.4% with SPX flat = hedges coming off into the MU print, even with net GEX still negative; the amplification regime is technically still ON but the fear that fed it is receding.
volatility_flow.note: No corroborated single large print. Cleanest flow read is structural: VIX -4.4% into the print, the 7,428 high rejected at the sub-flip resistance, the 7,350 put wall held on the dip.

technicals.breadth_note: Mixed/narrow. Dow green (+0.35%), Nasdaq red (-0.43%) — value over growth a third session. Portfolio movers (CBOE delayed_quotes, June 24 vs June 23 close): DELL +1.47% $434.06 (best held name), TSM +1.02% $440.83, ALAB +0.73% $399.92, AVGO +0.51% $382.07 (Jalapeño); NVDA -0.52% $199.00, GOOGL -0.24% $345.29, MAGS -0.73% $62.68, VRT -0.59% $316.43, MU -0.31% $1,048.51 (regular; +12% AH), MRVL -0.84% $276.70, CRWV -4.58% $100.88 (worst — broke below $105, neocloud/rate-sensitivity). Tracked: SOXX -0.31% $601.50, INTC -0.48% $131.65 (still above the $126 fade trigger; one session left on 2026-06-18-intel-apple-pop-fades). GOOGL $345.29 = THIRD straight close below Berkshire's $350 anchor (2026-06-01-googl-buffett-floor, falsifier = SUSTAINED closes below $350 — three closes now, getting close to 'sustained,' flag for review).
technicals.note: DMAs still drift-estimated (carried 10 sessions, stale) — refresh from a direct reading next run.

factor_attribution.dominant_driver: no clean intraday driver — a low-conviction fade ahead of the Micron print (the real driver landed AFTER the close: MU's blowout)
factor_attribution.detail: The regular session had no dominant macro driver — cross-asset was uniformly risk-stabilizing (10Y down, oil down, gold down, VIX down) yet equities couldn't hold a bounce, because the one catalyst that mattered (MU's 2027 guide) was after the bell. So the tape drifted: a green open, a failed poke at sub-flip resistance (7,428), an afternoon fade to flat-red. The FORWARD driver is now unambiguous: Micron's blowout (+12% AH, FQ4 guide $50B vs $43B Street, 16 take-or-pay SCAs) reframes the June 23 rout as a valuation reset and hands the memory/AI-hardware complex a fundamental bid into June 25. Secondary forward driver: PCE June 26. Cross-asset (factor_tape.py, Yahoo): 10Y 4.40% (-1.1%, falling — rate fear easing), WTI $69.87 (-4.56%, draining — disinflationary), DXY 101.58 (+0.17%), VIX 18.63 (-4.41%, fear bleeding out), gold $4,016 (-2.75%, dash-for-cash unwinding).

calendar.event: First post-print MU session + grading day for two open calls
calendar.why: MU +12% AH into June 25. Governs 2026-06-22-mu-sells-the-news (June 25 close vs June 24 close $1,048.51 — tracking toward WRONG on the gap) AND 2026-06-23-no-reclaim-7450 (last grading session — does the MU memory-complex gap carry SPX over the 7,450 flip?) AND 2026-06-18-intel-apple-pop-fades (last session; INTC $131.65, needs a close <$126 — unlikely) AND the new 2026-06-24-supply-leads-on-mu (semis outperform platforms on the MU read-through).

The week's macro fight, louder after BofA's 75bp-hike call but quieter after two days of falling oil/yields. Street ~3.4% y/y. Hot extends the rate-fear leg; soft is the broad relief. Governs 2026-06-22-floor-becomes-ceiling-holds (horizon), 2026-06-18-pce-duration-branch and 2026-06-23-pce-reaction (both conditional on a hot print).

calendar.event: Fed speakers (Williams, Goolsbee, Kashkari, Waller, Barkin)
calendar.why: First voices since the hawkish dot plot AND the BofA hike note. 'Remove the easing bias' tone pours fuel; 'data-dependent' is the relief. With oil/yields easing, the hawkish risk is fading at the margin.
GOOGL to close back above $350 by June 29 (closed $345.29 today, 3rd straight close below).
No SPCX close below $135 through June 30 (comfortably above the offer, ~$156).
Supply-side basket vs demand-side platforms from the June 1 baseline. The MU blowout is a fresh point FOR the supply side; the June 23 inverse day was the counter-print.

open_questions: NEW (the central June 25 question): does MU's +12% AH blowout gap the memory/AI-hardware complex up enough to carry SPX back over the 7,450 flip (re-stacking positive gamma, healing the regime to bull) — or does it stay a single-name/memory event with the broad index pinned below the flip into PCE? Governs 2026-06-23-no-reclaim-7450 (its falsifier is now armed by exactly this catalyst) and the new 2026-06-24-supply-leads-on-mu.

NEW: with the rout disambiguated as a valuation reset (MU guide raised, not cut) and the macro fear receding (oil sub-$70, 10Y 4.40%, VIX <19), is the June 23 low (7,336.82) the local bottom, or does PCE June 26 re-arm the downside? The cushion is technically still off (sub-flip close), so a hot PCE lands on amplify-mode plumbing.
CARRIED: does BofA's 75bp-of-2026-hikes call gain traction with Fed speakers / a hot PCE, or fade? Two days of falling oil and yields cut against it. Governs 2026-06-23-pce-reaction.

CARRIED (now 3 closes): 2026-06-01-googl-buffett-floor — GOOGL $345.29, THIRD straight close below Berkshire's $350 (falsifier = SUSTAINED closes below $350). Three closes; approaching 'sustained' — flag for user review. Note GOOGL held up RELATIVELY (-0.24%) again, complicating the 'platforms structurally lag' supply-over-demand read.

CARRIED (now a 5-session standing failure): market_levels.py --audit hung 0-bytes a 5TH straight day. Gamma snippet-only again (coherent with the tape). RECOMMEND formally retiring the dead trust-period audit and switching gamma to snippet-primary until the Polygon retry loop is fixed.

NEW (AVGO): the Jalapeño win vs the TPU v9 SerDes loss to MediaTek vs the $29B Apollo lease guarantee — does AVGO's custom-silicon leg (OpenAI) outweigh the socket loss (Google) and the contingent-liability overhang? Watch whether AVGO trades as a semi-leader or stays mega-cap-correlated into its next print.
